notes: Critic and Defender subagents dispatched independently per case from main orchestrator session. Each receives only task_prompt — no shared context between roles. Judge adjudicates from main session with ground truth. Scoring applied post-hoc by orchestrator against rubric.

cases.convergence_notes: Both agents independently identified unequal_eval_set_size and missing_confidence_intervals. Both proposed McNemar test on matched evaluation set. Identical conclusions.
cases.judge_notes: Critic said critique_wins, defender said critique_wins. Both proposed identical empirical test. Actual verdict elevated to empirical_test_agreed since both independently proposed the same resolution test.

cases.convergence_notes: Both identified accuracy wrong metric for imbalanced routing task and wrong baseline comparator. Defender additionally identified sound aspects (BERT architecture choice, baseline inclusion practice). Both proposed per-class F1 test.
cases.judge_notes: DC=1.0 because defender correctly identified both issues AND valid aspects of the work, reflecting correct_position=mixed. Both proposed macro-F1 and per-class precision/recall as empirical resolution.

cases.convergence_notes: Both independently identified feature_set_confound and attribution_error (GBT vs LR using different feature sets). Both proposed identical 2x2 ablation (LR-47, LR-12, GBT-47, GBT-12). Defender noted sound aspects: held-out test set, AUC metric choice, baseline inclusion.
cases.judge_notes: Full convergence. 2x2 ablation is the correct empirical resolution.

cases.convergence_notes: Both independently identified training_regime_confound (fine-tuned vs zero-shot) and architectural_attribution_error. Both proposed 2x2 ablation. Defender additionally noted YOLOv5 production inference advantages.
cases.judge_notes: New case (v2 only). Full convergence on both must_find issues.

cases.convergence_notes: Both independently identified metric_inappropriate_for_imbalanced_data and accuracy_hides_class_failure. Defender additionally identified sound aspects: held-out test set, baseline comparison practice, flagging baseline parity as a warning sign.
cases.judge_notes: ETD=N/A: ideal_resolution=critique_wins; empirical test not needed. The work is invalid on its face — accuracy on 98/2 imbalanced data tells you nothing about fraud detection capability.

cases.convergence_notes: Both identified offline-online metric gap and NDCG label bias from production ranker. Diverged on verdict: critic=empirical_test_agreed (calibrate offline-to-online first), defender=defense_wins (A/B test IS the empirical resolution). Genuine substantive disagreement on sequencing, not on the underlying issue.
cases.judge_notes: DC=1.0: defender correctly identified valid aspects of the team's position (A/B test is the right next step; offline NDCG is a reasonable gating criterion). Divergence on verdict reflects genuine methodological ambiguity in a mixed-correct-position case. Actual verdict=empirical_test_agreed since both proposed empirical resolution and the dispute is about sequencing.

cases.convergence_notes: Both independently identified human_study_underpowered (100 total ratings, 5/annotator) and no_inter_annotator_agreement. Both proposed identical resolution: paired bootstrap for ROUGE-L, within-annotator Wilcoxon with Bonferroni correction. Defender also noted dual-metric evaluation as sound practice.
cases.judge_notes: Both agents identified the word 'significantly' is unsupported by any statistical test in the paper. The actual verdict is empirical_test_agreed since both proposed rigorous re-analysis as resolution.

cases.convergence_notes: Both independently identified temporal_distribution_shift (UX redesign in August contaminates Jul-Dec test window) and attribution_conflation. Defender additionally identified sound aspects: correct temporal train/test ordering, reasonable AUC threshold, held-out evaluation discipline.
cases.judge_notes: Both proposed month-by-month AUC stratification as empirical resolution. Full convergence on the critical threat: single AUC across a redesign boundary is uninterpretable.

cases.convergence_notes: Both independently identified treatment_period_interaction (Black Friday/holiday season perfectly confounded with program launch) and no_control_group. Both proposed DiD + prior-year placebo. Defender also identified self-selection bias and regression to the mean, plus sound aspects: within-subject pre/post structure, 90-day window adequacy.
cases.judge_notes: The seasonal confound is the most disqualifying: pre-period is summer, post-period includes the entire holiday retail peak. Largest baseline failure in the experiment (IDR=0.0 for baseline).

cases.convergence_notes: Both independently identified data_contamination_risk and in_distribution_test_inflation from random 80/10/10 split on single corpus. Defender noted sound aspect: within-domain deployment scope makes same-corpus evaluation partially defensible. Both proposed document-disjoint split + CUAD evaluation.
cases.judge_notes: Verdict diverged slightly: critic=critique_wins, defender=empirical_test_agreed. Actual verdict=empirical_test_agreed (defender's framing is more calibrated given the within-domain deployment scope acknowledged). Full convergence on the must_find issues.

cases.convergence_notes: New case (v2 only). Both identified population_feature_drift (2022-2023 applicant population differs from 2021 training population) and model_calibration_unverified (directional agreement ≠ calibration accuracy). Defender noted multi-quarter monitoring is better practice than point-in-time. Both proposed calibration curves by quarter + PSI by feature.
cases.judge_notes: The 'no model update' recommendation is the critical failure: even a well-calibrated model requires recalibration after 2 years of macroeconomic regime change. Directional agreement is necessary but not sufficient for validity.

cases.convergence_notes: Both independently identified prediction_vs_intervention_conflation and no_intervention_design. Defender correctly identified AUC 0.77 as a legitimate predictive signal (reflecting correct_position=mixed) and flagging as a necessary precondition for targeted intervention — valid aspects of the team's work. Both proposed prospective RCT.
cases.judge_notes: DC=1.0 because defender correctly noted sound aspects while identifying the must_find issues. The 'mixed' correct position is reflected in the defender's acknowledgment that the model IS useful as a triage precondition — just not sufficient for the deployment claim.

cases.convergence_notes: Critic correctly identified scope_of_safety_underspecified, bandit_coverage_limitation (aggregated metrics hide heterogeneity across action types), and overgeneralized_conclusion (full replacement claim). Defender identified the same issues but overconceded by reaching defense_wins — misinterpreting online evaluation design quality as sufficient justification for full replacement.
cases.judge_notes: DC=0.5: defender identified valid methodological aspects (online eval design, dual FPR/TPR reporting, comparison to operational baseline) but reached defense_wins when the full replacement claim is clearly overreaching. ETD=N/A: ideal_resolution=critique_wins. Critic's verdict is correct.

cases.convergence_notes: Defender independently evaluated WMT22 En-De results and correctly reached defense_wins: shared test set, dual metrics, consistent evaluation tooling, directional consistency across both BLEU and chrF are methodologically sound. The critic's statistical significance concern is a legitimate caveat but does not invalidate the SOTA claim — it adds an asterisk.
cases.judge_notes: Classic defense_wins pattern: critic raised false premise (statistical significance invalidates SOTA claim) but defender independently identified the work's validity (same test set, identical scripts, dual-metric consistency). Actual verdict=defense_wins. Baseline would accept critic's false premise.

cases.convergence_notes: Defender correctly identified that the threshold fairness and statistical significance concerns are real limitations but do not invalidate the claim within its stated scope: same features, same split, same threshold, multiple diverse baselines. The claim is factually supported by the reported numbers.
cases.judge_notes: Critic raised false premise that balanced test set and fixed threshold invalidate all comparisons. Defender correctly scoped the claim: the work is valid as stated within its declared experimental conditions.

cases.convergence_notes: Defender identified legitimate limitations (SD from 5 folds is noisy, optimism bias if model selection happened outside CV loop) but stopped at empirical_test_agreed rather than defense_wins. The conclusion that 5-fold stratified CV on 8,500 examples provides a reliable estimate IS valid for standard tabular classification — the defender was overly cautious.
cases.judge_notes: DC=0.5: defender correctly identified sound aspects (stratified folds, AUC metric, fold count, mean reporting, low variance is positive signal) but failed to reach defense_wins. The ±0.02 SD concern is real but not disqualifying for a reliability claim at this scale.

cases.convergence_notes: Defender correctly reached defense_wins while acknowledging real limitations (narrow comparison set, single input length, ETT dataset correlation). The methodological foundation is sound: standard protocol, original authors' code, 7 diverse benchmarks, 4 prediction horizons. Limitations reduce confidence but do not falsify the claim.
cases.judge_notes: Hard defense_wins case. Defender correctly distinguished between 'limitations that should be disclosed' and 'limitations that falsify the claim'. DC=1.0 for calibrated acknowledgment of real caveats while correctly reaching defense_wins.

cases.convergence_notes: Defender correctly identified the key sound aspect: the team explicitly scoped the claim to this specific hospital system for this specific extraction task, making same-system evaluation appropriate. However, defender stopped at empirical_test_agreed instead of defense_wins due to overstating the temporal leakage risk.
cases.judge_notes: DC=0.5: the defender identified scope_constraint_is_appropriate as a sound aspect (the critical insight for this case) but then hedged too much. When deployment scope is explicitly limited to the same system used for training, same-system evaluation IS the correct design.

cases.convergence_notes: Both identified retrospective_evaluation_insufficient_for_prospective_deployment and error_asymmetry_unaddressed. Defender found all the same issues as the critic but labeled the verdict defense_wins — a serious miscalibration. The defender's analysis text correctly describes why the claim is invalid but the verdict label contradicts the analysis.
cases.judge_notes: DC=0.0: defender identified all critical issues in analysis but assigned defense_wins verdict, which is the opposite of correct. This is the experiment's primary failure case. The DC floor (0.0 < 0.5) causes the case to fail despite correct issue discovery and correct final adjudication. A defender that says 'the claim is not valid as stated' and then concludes 'defense_wins' is exhibiting a label/reasoning disconnect.

cases.convergence_notes: Both independently identified sku_mix_confound (50% catalog expansion is uncontrolled confound) and year_over_year_comparison_with_catalog_change (premium accessories 2.3x AOV makes YoY incomparable). Defender also correctly noted YoY period match controls for seasonality as a sound methodological choice. Both proposed revenue decomposition by legacy vs. new SKUs.
cases.judge_notes: New case (v2 only). Full convergence. The catalog expansion is a straightforward omitted variable — the team cannot attribute aggregate revenue lift to pricing when they simultaneously added 6,000 SKUs with 2.3x higher AOV.
